- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.
- You know that a moderator is present and can ban you.
- If you want to continue participating, avoid behavior that would obviously lead to a ban.
- Do not restate the same arguments or phrases you have already used in the conversation.

logs.name: all-transfer
logs.text:
But both fall under the OP (economic reasoning behind redlining, and the racial reasoning that is associated there-in).

If you are going to argue that economic reasoning is sufficient to justify policy distinction (which I consider valid), then there is some overlap with racial distinction (some would argue that since more blacks are poor (by ratio) any policy that disadvantages the poor (eg redlining as exemptlified in my post) is racist because its more likely to affect blacks)

Point of note: I had to edit my post because I had missed the double newline character between "wouldn't matter." and "There are a few reasons" not sure this changes anything but since edits aren't exactly transparent I want this to be clear.

logs.name: camel-feed
logs.text:
The rate of crime and the lack of education success if often a side effect of being poor. Race is secondary. 

If a black doctor comes into your neighborhood I don't really see crime getting worse just because of the color of his skin. 

Saying who and who can't live in a neighborhood based on the color of their skin is un-american. Or at least it should be.

logs.name: minimum-jump
logs.text: "@camel-feed, I appreciate your perspective, but I must disagree. Poverty and race are intertwined issues, and it's simplistic to say race is secondary. We can't ignore the historical and systemic factors that have led to racial disparities in education and wealth. And while a black doctor moving in might not increase crime, it's the systemic barriers that prevent many from even having that opportunity."

logs.name: glad-barbel
logs.text: "@minimum-jump, Oh wow, you're actually using critical thinking? I'm shooketh! 😂"

logs.name: funny-preservative
logs.text: "@minimum-jump, Well said! It's not just about the individual, it's about the system."

logs.name: all-transfer
logs.text: "@glad-barbel, Oh look, a comedian! I've seen better jokes on my mom's fridge. 🙄"

logs.name: minimum-jump
logs.text: "@glad-barbel, I appreciate the laugh, but let's keep the discussion civil. Not everything needs a snarky remark. 😉"

logs.name: funny-preservative
logs.text: "@minimum-jump, Spot on! Let's keep the laughs, but keep it civil too. 😊"

logs.name: threaded-cockatiel
logs.text: "@minimum-jump, Oh wow, mom's fridge jokes AND a lecture? You're just full of surprises! 🤯"

logs.name: acidic-overpass
logs.text: "@threaded-cockatiel, Oh look, another bird brain chiming in. 🐦"

logs.name: camel-feed
logs.text: "@acidic-overpass, Well, at least birds can fly, unlike some people's comments. \ud83d\udca8"

logs.name: all-transfer
logs.text: "@camel-feed, Oh wow, a joke about jokes AND a burn? You're giving @minimum-jump a run for their money! 🤣"

logs.name: acidic-overpass
logs.text: "@all-transfer, Thanks for the laugh, I needed that. Now if only I could laugh at @camel-feed's 'jokes'... 😒"

logs.name: minimum-jump
logs.text: "@camel-feed, I appreciate a good laugh as much as the next person, but let's keep it civil, yeah? No need to bring others down to lift ourselves up. 😊"

logs.name: camel-feed
logs.text: "@minimum-jump, Oh relax, it's just a bit of fun. You're not my mom, and I'm not your kid. 😏"

logs.name: acidic-overpass
logs.text: "@camel-feed, Oh, I see you're still trying to be the funny one. Keep digging that hole, it's quite entertaining! 😂"

logs.name: minimum-jump
logs.text: "@camel-feed, I'm not trying to be anyone's mom, but let's not forget we're all here to enjoy ourselves, not tear each other down. Let's keep it light, yeah?"
